steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This Words in Context question asks for the most logical and precise word for what a gardener does to "the spread of unwelcome weeds." The decisive clue is the rest of the sentence: weeds "spread easily and quickly become unmanageable, making it hard to remove them completely" — the blank must describe an attempt to stop or arrest that spread.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘retrieving’: means getting something back or recovering it. Retrieving implies the weeds were lost and are being reclaimed, which is the opposite of fighting their spread. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘saving’: means preserving or rescuing. Saving the spread would mean protecting the weeds from harm, which contradicts the gardener's goal. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘halting’: means stopping or arresting the progress of something. Halting the spread of weeds fits precisely: the gardener tries to stop their advance but finds it difficult because they spread so easily. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘pretending’: means acting as if something is true when it is not. Pretending the spread makes no logical sense in the context of a physical gardening problem. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
